events.title: The Jazz Mafia Celebrate the Music of Prince
events.description_preview: Prince Birthday Celebration Oakland’s powerhouse collective Jazz Mafia dives deep into the purple ca...
events.description_detail:
Prince Birthday Celebration
Oakland’s powerhouse collective Jazz Mafia dives deep into the purple catalog for a special tribute to the music of PRINCE. From slow-burning ballads to high-octane funk anthems, this show reimagines Prince’s iconic songs through the lens of jazz, soul, and hip-hop. Led by composer/trombonist Adam Theis and featuring the electrifying vocals of Kate Lamont, the ensemble brings fresh arrangements and deep grooves to this timeless repertoire.
Refunds up to 1 day before event

events.title: Rock & Roll Beer Stroll 2026 - Downtown Alameda
events.description_preview: Taste craft beers at unique locations in Downtown Alameda. Downtown Alameda Rock & Roll Beer Stroll ...
events.description_detail:
Taste craft beers at unique locations in Downtown Alameda.
Downtown Alameda Rock & Roll Beer Stroll - Saturday, June 13, 2026 (1-5pm)
Sip and stroll your way through our historic downtown district tasting local craft beers. Enjoy an afternoon filled with beer and entertainment while learning about the unique styles of Northern California breweries and local chefs.
Tickets include a collectible tasting glass, premium beer tastings at unique downtown locations, small bites at some locations and a commemorative guide.
Don’t wait! Ticket prices are as follows:
Rocker: Early Bird General Admission Ticket $50 limited available (then tickets $60 after early bird sells out, and $70 after June 7th - plus Eventbrite fees)
Roadie: Designated Driver Ticket $35 includes Non-Alcoholic Beers (plus Eventbrite fees)
VIP Tickets can be purchased HERE. Tickets include: VIP check in location at Dragon Rouge starting at 12pm, plenty of free parking, a seated VIP tasting experience and all items included with general tickets. More details and participating breweries to be announced!Sample beers from these local Bay Area craft breweries:
Alameda BrewingAlmanacBarebottle Brewing CoBrewBilt BrewingDrake's Brewing CompanyDTSJ BrewingEast Brother BeerFaction Brewing CompanyFathers BrewingHumble Sea Brewing Co.Temescal Brewing
And more to be announced soon!Learn more about the brewers and our fantastic hosts: www.AlamedaStrolls.com
Refunds up to 1 day before event

events.title: San Francisco Crystal Fair
events.description_preview: Crystals, minerals, beads, jewelry, metaphysical healing tools. Something for everyone. The Great Sa...
events.description_detail:
Crystals, minerals, beads, jewelry, metaphysical healing tools. Something for everyone.
The Great San Francisco Crystal Fair is taking place on June 13-14 at the San Francisco County Fair Building in Golden Gate Park. It will host the largest collection of crystals, beads, minerals jewelry and metaphysical healing tools that you’ve ever seen in our fair city. Thousands of items will be available at excellent prices, plus you can meet practitioners of the healing arts.
Over 50 exhibiters will dazzle you with their vast storehouse of brilliant gemstones and shamanic wisdom. They include vendors from around the country and exotic places like Bali, Nepal, Afghanistan and China. Come explore the vast delights and many splendors of the Great San Francisco Crystal Fair
Refunds up to 7 days before event
San Francisco, CA 94122

events.title: 2026 San Francisco Affordable Housing Expo
events.description_preview: Annual Housing Resource Fair for renters, homebuyers, and homeowners! Join us for the 2026 San Franc...
events.description_detail:
Annual Housing Resource Fair for renters, homebuyers, and homeowners!
Join us for the 2026 San Francisco Affordable Housing Fair & Help Center!
Learn about affordable housing resources and education in San Francisco presented by HUD-approved housing counseling agencies, the San Francisco Mayor's Office of Housing and Community Development (MOHCD) and industry professionals.
CONNECT WITH EXPERTS - Non-profit organizations, City housing services, and local real estate professionals available, in-person, to answer your housing questions and provide valuable information.
And visit the HELP CENTER for individual assistance and support with your personal housing challenges, including:To apply for SF affordable housing programs, eligibility review, credit and debt issues, eviction defense, financial planning, estate planning, foreclosure prevention and more.
Support for unhoused individuals and families is available.
And check out our FREE workshops at the Expo (see schedule below)
We also have FREE ONLINE WORKSHOPS June 2 thru June 11th:
CLICK HERE FOR ONLINE WORKSHOP SCHEDULE
Go to www.homesanfrancisco.org for more information.

events.title: MEMBA at Public Works
events.description_preview: Saturday, June 13, 9:30pm - 2am Main Room / 21+ / Limited Capacity Want free entry? Check out the PW...
events.description_detail:
Saturday, June 13, 9:30pm - 2am
Main Room / 21+ / Limited Capacity
Want free entry? Check out the PW Union where members get event access, drinks, skip the line, and more: www.publicsf.com/membership
Curious about more ticket options, deals, and ways to party? Check out our website at www.publicsf.com
Brooklyn duo MEMBA bring their world-bending electronic sound to Public Works. Known for fusing ancient global rhythms with future bass and trap, they've graced stages from Coachella to Glastonbury and released on ODESZA's Foreign Family Collective. Expect a journey.
day astro b2b pxlwlkr
MEMBA is the Brooklyn-based electronic duo of Ishaan Chaudhary and Will Curry. The two met on an archaeological dig in India and started making music together in 2014, drawing from Chaudhary's Bollywood and Sufi roots and Curry's background in percussion. The result is a cinematic sound that weaves ancient world textures into heavy, melodic bass music. They've released on ODESZA's Foreign Family Collective, Monstercat, and 88rising, and their track "For Aisha" charted globally after being featured in a Bollywood film that premiered at TIFF. Their latest album ABOVEGROUND* dropped in late 2025. Festival appearances include Coachella, EDC, Glastonbury, Electric Forest, Shambhala, and Bonnaroo.
San Francisco, CA 94103

events.title: Eva zu Beck's THE WILDER WAY Off-Site Event And Book Signing
events.description_preview: Famed adventurer zu Beck shares her fearless new travel memoir packed with inspiration for leading a...
events.description_detail:
Famed adventurer zu Beck shares her fearless new travel memoir packed with inspiration for leading a more vibrant, wild, and authentic life
Join us at Mrs. Dalloway's Bookstore on Friday, June 12 at 7:00 PM when we host internationally beloved YouTube adventurer and National Geographic TV host Eva zu Beck at The David Brower Center in downtown Berkeley. Eva willto share her new memoir The Wilder Way: A Memoir of Adventure, Freedom, and an Uncharted Life. She will be joined in conversation by Caroline Paul and will sign copies of her book after the presentation.
Click here to preorder a copy of The Wilder Way.
Are you living with a capital “L”? When was the last time you truly felt you were your wildest, most free, most alive self? Eva zu Beck, professional storyteller and avid adventurer believes our best life is available to each of us, in every moment. But we have to seize it. The comfort we crave can also be “a silent killer of dreams.” Whether your dream is to scale a foreign mountain, hike a local trail, or pick up a paint brush, Eva’s message is clear: The world belongs to the brave.
Through heart-pounding adventures and raw, intimate storytelling, Eva reveals how stepping off life’s conventional highway—what she calls the path of “mortgage, marriage, lineage”—opened her world to experiences she never imagined possible: learning to trek solo with horses across Mongolia, surviving three months alone on a desert island in Yemen during COVID, driving an eighteen-year-old truck from Mexico to Alaska, and attempting a 300-mile ultramarathon in the Arctic Circle. But this isn’t just a collection of daring feats. Each chapter not only details a life-altering experience but reveals the hidden lessons Eva learned along the way. It’s a blueprint for anyone who feels trapped by society’s expectations and longs to create a life of genuine adventure, connection, and meaning.

events.title: Connect with Clay! Monthly Artist-led Air-Dry Clay Workshops
events.description_preview: Connect with Clay! Monthly Artist-led Air-Dry Clay Workshops at the Palo Alto Art Center FREE Connec...
events.description_detail:
Connect with Clay!  Monthly Artist-led Air-Dry Clay Workshops at the Palo Alto Art Center
FREE
Connect with Clay! Monthly Artist-led Air-Dry Clay Workshops at the Palo Alto Art Center FREE Connect with Clay! Monthly Artist-led Air-Dry Clay Workshops at the Palo Alto Art Center FREE – Made Possible by Palo Alto Art Center Foundation Every second Friday of the month (November 14, December 12, January 9, February 13, March 13, April 10, May 8, June 12, July 17*) 6- 8 p.m.
All materials provided
All ages; children under 12 must be accompanied by an adult. Each child will require their own ticket.
Space is limited, advance registration required for each individual session.
Please RSVP for each guest attending!
Come together around clay in community at a series of creative workshops led by Centering exhibiting artists Timna Naim and Larisa Usich. Each workshop will feature different projects inspired by themes in the current exhibitions at the Art Center. Visit the exhibition after hours with an exclusive tour by the teaching artists, sketch out your ideas, and then make your own creation in clay that you can take home, and contribute to a collective artwork creation for the Center.
Throughout the series, Timna and Larisa will explore how small-scale decorations can hold powerful meaning, exploring the history of trinkets, miniatures and other forms of adornment as important tools of self-expression.
Bring your friends, and come ready to learn, explore, and create!
Timna Naim, a San Jose-based artist, explores identity, play, and community through interdisciplinary work integrating ceramic sculpture, installation, and performance that centers on tactility, storytelling, and participation, using vibrant colors, abstract forms, and interactive elements to spark the imagination. Timna Naim holds an MFA in Spatial Art and an MA in Teaching from SJSU.

The event will take place at the Greenmeadow clubhouse. You can park at the Greenmeadow pool and follow signs to the event.

*We suggest a donation of $15 to cover food and other meetup expenses. Donations can be made at the event with cash, check or venmo.
Please register if you plan to attend so that we can plan the amount of food and drinks.

events.title: Volunteer Outdoors in Palo Alto at Pearson-Arastradero Preserve
events.description_preview: Volunteer your time with Grassroots Ecology to help increase biodiversity in Palo Alto! Volunteer wi...
events.description_detail:
Volunteer your time with Grassroots Ecology to help increase biodiversity in Palo Alto!
Volunteer with Grassroots Ecology to support the expansive oak woodlands and grasslands at Pearson-Arastradero Preserve. These ecosystems are home to an abundance of wildlife, including deer, bobcats, coyotes, many species of birds and insects, and more! During our time together, volunteers will remove invasive plants from restoration areas using hand tools, bucket water native plants, and sheet mulch areas to suppress weeds. Activities may involve lifting, bending, and walking on flat and uneven terrain.
Visit Grassroots Ecology’s Pearson-Arastradero Preserve webpage to learn more about the ecosystem and accessibility considerations.
Waivers/Registration: All volunteers attending the workday must be registered individually and have approved the online waiver through this Eventbrite page. Youth under 18 must have their legal guardian approve the waiver on their behalf. Volunteers age 12 and under require an accompanying adult.
Parking: 1530 Arastradero Road, Palo Alto. Please arrive early and carpool if possible. There will be some parking spaces reserved for the event at the back of the lot. There is no street parking available along Arastradero Road or Page Mill Road.
Alternate Transportation: Bike racks are available near the parking lot. The 280/Page Mill Rd Park and Ride lot is a 10-minute walk from the preserve.
Meeting Space: We will meet by the buildings down the path from the parking lot. Water fountains and all-gender, single stall restrooms are accessible at the meeting spot and within a 10 minute walk during the event.
Prepare for Working Outdoors:
We will provide gloves, tools, and snacks.
We are committed to the safety of our staff and volunteers. If we must cancel a workday due to extreme weather conditions or poor air quality, a Grassroots Ecology staff person will reach out via email no later than 5:00 PM the day before this event.

events.title: Summer Kickoff Activity Fair at The Plex
events.description_preview: Free family activity fair with sports demos, fitness experiences, youth programs, vendors & interact...
events.description_detail:
Free family activity fair with sports demos, fitness experiences, youth programs, vendors & interactive activities at The Plex!
Celebrate summer at The Plex with a free community event designed for new and prospective members to explore everything our facility has to offer.
The Plex is a one-of-a-kind destination for sports, fitness, family activities, dining, events, and entertainment, featuring 240,000 square feet of experiences all under one roof. From youth sports and fitness programs to birthday parties, camps, recovery services, dining, and more, there’s something for everyone to discover.
The Plex Activity Fair: Summer Kickoff brings together interactive sports demos, fitness experiences, youth programs, vendor booths, and hands-on activities for all ages. Explore hockey, soccer, aquatics, group fitness, personal training, recovery services, summer camps, birthday parties, and more while meeting the teams behind each department.
Guests can enjoy skill challenges, mini class demos, music, interactive experiences, and opportunities to learn more about programs throughout the facility. Participants will also have the opportunity to take part in an interactive Event Passport Challenge by visiting activities and booths throughout the event.
This event is the perfect opportunity to experience what The Plex community is all about while taking advantage of exclusive new member incentives and event-day promotions available only during the Summer Kickoff Activity Fair.
Registration is free but encouraged for easier event check-in.

events.description_preview: Kicking off World Cup weekend under the stars with Noodles. Patio energy, community, and sound all c...
events.description_detail:
Kicking off World Cup weekend under the stars with Noodles. Patio energy, community, and sound all collide at SP2.
Heavy Arsenal + SP2 Communal Bar & Restaurant Present
Under the stars, on the patio, where music and community collide.
Born in San Francisco and raised in Hayward, Micah, aka NOODLES grew up in a Filipino American household where success was expected, but it was the local club scene that sparked her passion. Taught by her father, a former resident DJ, she mastered analog skills and translated them into the digital world, quickly making a name for herself across California. With viral SoundCloud edits and sets at major festivals like Camp Flog Gnaw, Wireless, and Soulection Experience, NOODLES has become a standout solo act with a style all her own.
Bottle Service (415)737.5772
Ticketed Event. NO REFUNDS.

events.title: VIP Tickets Rock & Roll Beer Stroll 2026 - Downtown Alameda
events.description_preview: The VIP session will highlight the incredible connection between Alameda's very own Admiral Maltings...
events.description_detail:
The VIP session will highlight the incredible connection between Alameda's very own Admiral Maltings and the brewing process.
VIP Beer Tasting Experience - Saturday, June 13, 2026
Select this VIP experience before the main stroll, from 12-1pm at Dragon Rouge.​Limited to just 50 attendees, this exclusive session offers a fascinating look into Alameda’s unique role in the beer world. The VIP session will highlight the incredible connection between Alameda’s very own Admiral Maltings and the brewing process. Discover how Admiral Maltings contributes to the creation of many of the beers you’ll savor throughout the evening. In addition to insights into the artistry behind the beer-making process, you’ll also have the opportunity to sample unique beers from their brewery partners. This is your chance to dive deeper into Alameda’s rich beer connections while enjoying an intimate, educational, and flavorful start to the afternoon.This $75 ticket will include a seated VIP program (from 12:15 - 1pm), a collectible tasting glass, premium beer tastings at unique downtown locations, delicious bites at some host locations prepared by local chefs and a commemorative guide. Tickets are limited, price increases to $85 on June 8th, if not sold out.After the VIP session join the Rock & Roll Beer Stroll (1:00-5:00pm) and walk around downtown Alameda and sample beers from local breweries.
For general admission tickets please visit Downtown Alameda Rock & Roll Stroll.

events.title: Scopo Divino: Winemaker Dinner with St. Francis Winery
events.description_preview: Tickets Available Now for Scopo Divino's 3-Course Winemaker Dinner featuring St. Francis Winery, Tue...
events.description_detail:
Tickets Available Now for Scopo Divino's 3-Course Winemaker Dinner featuring St. Francis Winery, Tuesday, June 9th at 5:30 & 8:00pm
Tuesday, June 9th with Two Seatings: 5:30pm & 8:00pm
St. Francis & Katie Madigan
It's hard to describe how excited we are to have St. Francis Winery here at Scopo Divino.  I personally have a handful of remarkable experiences from my visits there. Some that shifted how I approach wine. One that helps define how we do our wine club here. To give full weight to their importance to us here:  they have been featured on our mural since our opening. It was so fun to taste for this event with their President & Ceo, Rick Bonitati and see the excitement on his face when we he saw the mural. Let's just say this one is a long time coming, and a true culmination of our relationship to wine here at Scopo Divino.
This June, Scopo Divino is proud to present "Best Woman Winemaker" award winner, Katie Madigan and St. Francis Winery. We will be experiencing four of their exceptional wines, paired with an amazing three-course meal crafted by celebrity guest Chef and legend, Erik Hopfinger.
This will be a Three Course Tasting Meal with Wines Paired for each course, and will include the opportunity to meet and engage with Katie Madigan herself. As guests at the dinner you will have access to special reserve one-night-only pricing on bottles of the wines to go.
Pairing wine & food is an art, and these dinners are built in reverse. As in we taste and select the wine first, THEN craft the meal around them. In this way you'll come away with an excellent understanding of the wines - how you might drink, serve, share, and cook with them. (Full menu details including vegetarian options will be featured in the weeks to come).
The first seating will be at 5:30pm, the second at 8:00pm. We show guests to their tables 15 minutes prior to each seating and start promptly at the times listed.

events.description_preview: Comal welcomes ArteNOM for a multi-course meal featuring their tequilas paired with dishes by Comal ...

events.title: A Taste of Willow Glen
events.description_preview: Taste your way through Willow Glen's best spots on Lincoln Ave! Every dollar collected helps the Wil...
events.description_detail:
Taste your way through Willow Glen's best spots on Lincoln Ave! Every dollar collected helps the Willow Glen Lions Club serve the community!
Get ready for a fantastic evening along Lincoln Avenue in Willow Glen! Munch on delicious samplers from popular eateries, compare notes with your friends, and plan a meal together at your favorites from the day.

events.title: Family Fun: Roman Style Pizza Making Class (Parent + Child)
events.location: 2855 Stevens Creek Boulevard, San Jose, CA 95128
events.description_preview: Bring your bambini and get ready to cook! In our hands-on format, our Scuola Chef will guide you thr...
events.description_detail:
Bring your bambini and get ready to cook! In our hands-on format, our Scuola Chef will guide you through how to prepare Pizza alla Pala.
About this Class: Bring your bambini and get ready to cook! In our hands-on format, you’ll be diving right in and making our signature recipe with your child from scratch. Take your culinary creation with you at the end of class and enjoy your own hard work at home! Our Scuola Chef will then prepare the dish for you to taste in class.
About this Dish: Welcome to the world of pizza! There are many different types of pizza across Italy. Coming from Rome, Pizza alla Pala is an oval-shaped pizza served on a wooden pala, or paddle. Thick and crispy on the outside, but still fluffy in the center, the toppings are added after the pizza is baked. In this class, you’ll make your own pizza dough from scratch with your bambino.
In this Class You Will:
Payment in full is required at the time of registration and all sales are final. No refunds are offered on classes and events. For groups of 5-10 guests, a minimum of 7 days' notice (class day not included) is required to receive a credit in the form of a coupon for a future class or event. For groups of up to 4 guests, a minimum of 4 days' notice (class day not included) is required to receive a credit in the form of a coupon code for a future class or event at La Scuola. We reserve the right to reschedule or cancel a class or event due to unforeseen circumstances, inclement weather or otherwise. In the event of a class or event cancellation, we will contact you directly at the phone number or email address you have provided to offer a full refund or credit to transfer to a future class or event. While we are not able to offer credit, transfers or refunds for last-minute illnesses, work-related conflicts, personal emergencies or events beyond our control, you are always welcome to send someone in your place, as we understand that these things do happen.

events.title: Cyb3r South Bay 6.13 // Industrial, EBM, Darkwave, & Dark Techno
events.description_preview: Cyb3r South Bay hits X-Bar Cupertino June 13 with industrial, EBM, darkwave & dark techno DJs. 18+ |...
events.description_detail:
Cyb3r South Bay hits X-Bar Cupertino June 13 with industrial, EBM, darkwave & dark techno DJs. 18+ | 8PM–12AM | Dark vibes await.
Step into the underground at CYB3R South Bay, a night dedicated to the darker side of electronic music featuring Industrial, EBM, Darkwave, and Dark Techno sounds at X-Bar in Cupertino. Taking place on Saturday, June 13, 2026, this 18+ event brings together heavy beats, dark aesthetics, and immersive club energy for fans of alternative electronic culture.
Headlining the night is Plastic Disease, known for delivering aggressive industrial rhythms, distorted synth textures, and high-energy sets that blend classic EBM influences with modern dark techno sounds. Their performances create a raw, hypnotic atmosphere built for the dance floor.
Also performing is C_Death, whose sets fuse darkwave melodies, cyber-industrial soundscapes, and driving basslines into a moody and cinematic experience. Expect deep cuts, underground favorites, and relentless energy throughout the night.
Joining the lineup is Hopeless (OAK), bringing a darker and more atmospheric approach with haunting synths, post-industrial beats, and emotionally charged selections that pull from goth, dark electronic, and techno influences.
The event takes place at X-Bar Cupertino, located at 20990 Homestead Rd, from 8PM–12AM. Tickets are $10 in advance and $13 at the door. Whether you’re into industrial dance music, goth club culture, or underground techno scenes, CYB3R South Bay promises a night of dark sounds and late-night energy.
Refunds up to 7 days before event
